briefdescription.para: This definitions specify the EXTRA bytes in each of the command transactions.

detaileddescription.para: This count includes Command byte, address bytes and any don't care bytes needed.

briefdescription.para: This function enables writes to the STM Serial Flash memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Spi device.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the data to the specified locations in the STM Serial Flash memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Spi device.
is the address in the Buffer, where to write the data.
is the number of bytes to be written.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from the STM Serial Flash Memory.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Spi device.
is the starting address in the Flash Memory from which the data is to be read.
is the number of bytes to be read.

briefdescription.para: This function erases the entire contents of the STM Serial Flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Spi device.

simplesect.para: The erased bytes will read as 0xFF.

briefdescription.para: This function erases the contents of the specified Sector in the STM Serial Flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Spi device.
is the address within a sector of the Buffer, which is to be erased.

simplesect.para: The erased bytes will be read back as 0xFF.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Status register of the STM Flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the Spi device.

simplesect.para: The status register content is stored at the second byte pointed by the ReadBuffer.

briefdescription.para: This function waits till the STM serial Flash is ready to accept next command.

simplesect.para: This function reads the status register of the Buffer and waits . till the WIP bit of the status register becomes 0.

briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which performs processing for the SPI driver.

detaileddescription.para: It is called from an interrupt context such that the amount of processing performed should be minimized. It is called when a transfer of SPI data completes or an error occurs.
This handler provides an example of how to handle SPI interrupts and is application specific.

parameterdescription.para: is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
is the event that just occurred.
is the number of bytes transferred up until the event occurred.

briefdescription.para: Main function to execute the Flash example.
This file contains a design example using the SPI driver (XSpi) and hardware device with an STM serial Flash device (M25P series) in the interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: This example erases a Sector, writes to a Page within the Sector, reads back from that Page and compares the data.
This example has been tested with an M25P16 device. The bytes per page (STM_PAGE_SIZE) in M25P16 is 256.
This example assumes that the underlying processor is MicroBlaze.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a sd 02/26/08 First release 3.00a ktn 10/28/09 Converted all register accesses to 32 bit access. Updated to use the HAL APIs/macros. Replaced call to XSpi_Initialize API with XSpi_LookupConfig and XSpi_CfgInitialize. 4.2 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. ms 04/05/17 Modified Comment lines to follow doxygen rules. 4.11 sb 07/11/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.
